title: The Lego Adventures of Mistress Mouse and Other Mouse
summary: Mistress Mouse decides to skip a day of adventure to stay at home, parking her little pink truck in the garage. She spends her afternoon building Lego structures for her stuffy friends—Mickey, Oscar, and Elfie—before enjoying a massive feast of Indian food, guacamole, and chips. After reading a bedtime story, she falls asleep to a gentle lullaby, waking up the next day to play in her yard. Meanwhile, Other Mouse enjoys a peaceful day building with Legos, showing resilience by rebuilding a funny tower after it falls and sharing his success with his friend Mickey Mouse.

highlightQuote.text: She had rice and naan and pork and after the dipping sauce and guacamole and chips.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we're gonna use the lantern for stars. That's our new way of doing stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ready? Here come the stars. Three, two, one...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need my special flashlight. It's so dark!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It is dark. I'll turn the light back on. Do you like the stars?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I need my flashlight for one minute, and then we can tell stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I've gotta find it, my love... Oh, there it is.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is your phone in there?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's not, actually, because now we have the lantern that can make the light. So I don't have to use my phone. Okay, here's the flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now it's time to tell the story. You may borrow my flashlight for the story. You may do the story, Dad.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I would love to do a story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tell a story, Dada! Tell a story, Dada. About a mouse called Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a story about a mouse called Mistress Mouse? Once upon a time, Mistress Mouse was in her house...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And she parked her truck in her garage. Her little pink truck outside in her driveway, and it wasn't driving anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it wasn't driving anymore. Why not?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, because she parked it to go at home, to play at home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did she like to play at home?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With her toys.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was her favorite toy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: All kinds of toys. All the toys. She liked building with her Legos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did she build out of Legos?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Something for her little mouse friend.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who is her mouse friend?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Her stuffy mice. I mean, stuffy mice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, her stuffy mice. What were their names?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mickey, Oscar, Elfie, and Oscar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. Well, on that day, Mistress Mouse had a special adventure to go on. What was the adventure she was going to go on?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She wasn't going to go on any adventure.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No? She was just going to stay home?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, she was going to stay home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, after she played for a long time, it was time to have dinner. What did she decide to have for dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Indian food.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Indian food! What did she have for her Indian food?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She had rice and naan and pork and after the dipping sauce and guacamole and chips.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! She ate it... and then she ate it. That sounds like a delicious meal. What did she do after dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She played some more, and then it was time for bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and then it was time to read the last book. And what was her last book that she read?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She read 'How to Catch a Star'.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She read 'How to Catch a Star'. And then she sang her lullaby. What was her lullaby? How did that go?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Little Mousey baby, uh-uh-uh-uh. Little Mousey baby, uh-uh-uh-uh. It goes like this: uh-uh-uh-uh-uh-uh-uh-uh. It goes like this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a beautiful lullaby. And then she was all ready to sleep. So she got in her bed, and she got a hug and kiss from her Dada.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then he... and then she went to sleep in her pillow and covered it all up and slept. And then when the other morning, she woke up and played outside in her yard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And that is the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want another story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but Izzy, it's almost time to go to sleep, little love.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I want to tell her last story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, one more short story. This is going to be a story about Other Mouse. Once upon a time, Other Mouse had a problem. What was his problem?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He didn't have any problem.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he didn't have any problem? Oh. What was he doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He was playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was he playing with?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Legos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was he making with Legos?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He was making a little funny building.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he made a little funny building. But then, bonk! He accidentally knocked it over. So what did he do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He built it again!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He built it again! And then he decided to show it to his friend. Which friend did he show it to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Showed it to his other mouse friend called Mickey Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The end. All righty.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Oscar
characters.description: Hunkered in velvety shadows beside one of Mistress Mouse’s tattered stuffy mice, Lily’s brother illuminates a salvaged television with a scavenged flashlight, contentedly nibbling on oily debris. His focus drifts from the flickering screen to the miniature green car mired in a mud puddle—a casualty of the same reckless speed he employs when racing his yellow school bus. Between these high-stakes heats, he engineers sturdy Lego bridges that arch over the surrounding wreckage, all under the unblinking watch of his glass-eyed, plush sentinel.

characters.name: Elfie
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse's stuffy mice.

characters.name: Other Mouse
characters.description: A spirited mouse racer who navigates grueling long-distance courses through tangled jungles and exotic zoos with a carefree heart. Unburdened by any worldly troubles, he finds bliss in the adrenaline of the track and the quiet precision of building with Legos, living a life of seamless, architectural joy.

characters.name: Mickey Mouse
characters.description: Minnie’s musical companion and star-bound co-pilot is an industrious toy mouse who returns from school to mend the broken and fill the silence of the void with song. While he remains Izzy’s imagined confidant—a silent listener to her whispered anxieties regarding dwindling supplies—he also finds joy in companionship, proudly showcasing his intricate Lego creations to a trusted friend amidst the stars.

places.name: Mistress Mouse's House
places.description: Mistress Mouse’s home base is a vibrant sanctuary where high-octane preparation meets playful domesticity. While it serves as the bustling staging ground for her iconic pink tow truck, it is also a private retreat where she tinkers with her toys and enjoys a quiet dinner. This pre-race haven seamlessly blends meticulous engine fine-tuning with the comforts of home, fueling her spirit long before the roar of competition begins.

places.name: Garage
places.description: The place where Mistress Mouse parks her little pink truck.

places.name: Driveway
places.description: The area outside Mistress Mouse's house where the pink truck sits when parked.

places.name: Yard
places.description: The outdoor area where Mistress Mouse plays the morning after her big dinner.
